tests.output_sample:
Reads a sender list from --from-senders (file path, or '-' for stdin — one
address per line), resolves matching messages in the local store, and prints
the move plan (count per sender, total count, target folder). Without
--execute, no API calls are made; with --execute, the command iterates ids
and calls POST /me/messages/{id}/move with the destinationId.

The default behavior is print-only — opt-in to actual mutation with --execute.
Short-circuits under PRINTING_PRESS_VERIFY=1 so verify subprocesses never
mutate the mailbox.

Usage:
  outlook-email-pp-cli bulk-archive [flags]

Examples:
# Plan only — no API calls
  outlook-email-pp-cli bulk-archive --from-senders senders.txt --to-folder Archive --agent

  # Pipe a quiet-senders output as the sender list, plan only
  outlook-email-pp-cli quiet --baseline 90d --silent 30d --agent --select sender \
    | jq -r '.items[].sender' \
    | outlook-email-pp-cli bulk-archive --from-senders - --to-folder Archive --agent

  # Execute the move
  outlook-email-pp-cli bulk-archive --from-senders senders.txt --to-folder Archive --execute --agent

Flags:
      --db string             Database path
      --execute               Actually call POST /me/messages/{id}/move per id (default: print plan only)
      --from-query string     Reserved: FTS query producing a sender list (not yet implemented)
      --from-senders string   Path to a sender list (one address per line, or '-' for stdin)
  -h, --help                  help for bulk-archive
      --limit int             Cap the number of messages considered (safety net for huge mailboxes)
      --since string          Limit to messages received after this point (e.g. '90d', '2026-01-01', 'last-sync')

tests.output_sample:
Groups local messages by the chosen dimension (--by) and returns groups with
more than one row. Modes:

  conversation     group by conversation_id (cross-folder copies of the same thread)
  message-id       group by internet_message_id (same RFC 822 message in multiple folders)
  subject-sender   group by LOWER(subject) + from address + LOWER(to addresses)

`subject-sender` is the noisiest mode and intentional — newsletters often arrive
with identical subjects but different message-ids; this catches them.

  outlook-email-pp-cli dedup [flags]

  outlook-email-pp-cli dedup --by subject-sender --top 50 --agent

Aggregates the local messages store for a single calendar date (default: today
in the local time zone). Output covers:

  - received_count / sent_count / unread_count / flagged_count
  - top_senders[--top]  (sender, count, unread)
  - top_conversations[--top]  (subject, message_count, last_from)
  - focused_ratio       (count_focused / received_count)
  - has_attachments_count
  - flagged_completed_count

Joins local messages where from.emailAddress.address = me (resolved from the
sent folder) against later messages in the SAME conversation_id whose from
address matches one of the original recipients.

A sent message qualifies as "needs followup" when:
  - it was sent more than --days ago (default 7)
  - to a specific recipient (or any if --to is empty)
  - and there is NO later message in the same conversation from any of its
    recipients

No Graph endpoint exposes this; the local store is what makes the cross-thread
join possible.

Usage:

Examples:

tests.output_sample:
Sync data from the API into a local SQLite database. Supports resumable
incremental sync (only fetches new data since last sync) and full resync.
Once synced, use the 'search' command for instant full-text search.

Exit codes & warnings:
  Resources the API denies access to (HTTP 403, or HTTP 400 with an
  access-policy body) are reported as warnings rather than failing the
  run. In --json mode each is emitted as a {"event":"sync_warning",...}
  line carrying status, reason, and message fields, and a final
  {"event":"sync_summary",...} aggregates the run.

  Exit 0 when at least one resource synced and no resource flagged in
  the spec as critical (x-critical: true) failed; non-critical failures
  emit {"event":"sync_warning","reason":"exit_policy_default_changed",
  ...} so callers can detect that a partial failure was tolerated. Pass
  --strict to exit non-zero on any per-resource failure. Exit is always
  non-zero when every selected resource failed, regardless of --strict.

Usage:
  outlook-email-pp-cli sync [flags]

Examples:
  # Sync all resources
  outlook-email-pp-cli sync

  # Sync specific resources only
  outlook-email-pp-cli sync --resources channels,messages

  # Full resync (ignore previous checkpoint)
  outlook-email-pp-cli sync --full

  # Incremental sync: only records from the last 7 days
  outlook-email-pp-cli sync --since 7d

  # Parallel sync with 8 workers
  outlook-email-pp-cli sync --concurrency 8

  # Latest-only: refresh head of each resource, no historical backfill
  outlook-email-pp-cli sync --latest-only

Flags:
      --concurrency int     Number of parallel sync workers (default 4)
      --db string           Database path (default: ~/.local/share/outlook-email-pp-cli/data.db)
      --full                Full resync (ignore previous checkpoint)
  -h, --help                help for sync
      --latest-only         Refresh head of each resource only; clears resume cursor and caps pages at 1. Mutually exclusive with --since (--since wins).
      --max-pages int       Maximum pages to fetch per resource (0 = unlimited; cap-hit emits a sync_warning event) (default 100)
      --resources strings   Comma-separated resource types to sync
      --since string        Incremental sync duration (e.g. 7d, 24h, 1w, 30m)
      --strict              Exit non-zero on any per-resource failure (default: only critical failures or all-resource failure exit non-zero).

      --config string        Config file path
      --csv                  Output as CSV (table and array responses)

      --dry-run              Show request without sending

      --ignore-missing       Treat missing delete targets as a successful no-op

      --no-cache             Bypass response cache
      --no-color             Disable colored output
      --no-input             Disable all interactive prompts (for CI/agents)
      --plain                Output as plain tab-separated text
      --profile string       Apply values from a saved profile (see 'outlook-email-pp-cli profile list')
      --quiet                Bare output, one value per line
      --rate-limit float     Max requests per second (0 to disable)
      --select string        Comma-separated fields to include in output (e.g. --select id,name,status)

      --yes                  Skip confirmation prompts (for agents and scripts)
